info.title: Wharf Backend API
info.description:
Zero-knowledge sync backend for Wharf. The server only ever stores
ciphertext vault blobs and bcrypt hashes of client-derived credential
keys. It never sees passwords or plaintext vault contents.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: Google / GitHub social login (links an external identity, then issues the app's own JWTs)

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Account profile, public key and incoming project invites

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Zero-knowledge team workspaces (server stores ciphertext + metadata only)

tags.name: Vault
tags.description: Encrypted vault blob storage (server stores ciphertext only)

tags.name: Device Codes
tags.description: Pair the terminal client with an account

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Account registration, login, refresh and recovery

get.summary: Fetch the stored vault blob and its version

put.summary: Replace the vault blob with optimistic concurrency
Publish or rotate the account's X25519 public key
put.description: Rotating (rotate=true) replaces the key and resets every wrapped key the account holds — each affected project membership re-enters the awaiting-key state.

409.description: A key already exists and rotate was not set

get.summary: Fetch the project vault and the caller's wrapped DEK (any member)

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member

put.summary: Replace the project vault with optimistic concurrency (keyed member)

403.description: Caller holds no wrapped key yet

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member
Invite not found or not addressed to the caller

post.summary: Accept a project invite, joining as an awaiting-key member

404.description: Invite not found or not addressed to the caller

post.summary: Create a project with its initial vault and the owner's wrapped DEK

412.description: Caller has not published a public key

post.summary: Rotate the project DEK and re-wrap it for all remaining members (admin+)

200.description: Rotated; returns the new vault version

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member

403.description: Caller is not an admin, or an admin removing an admin

post.summary: Seal the project DEK to a member's public key (admin+)

409.description: Vault version has moved on (stale DEK)

post.summary: Invite an email to the project (admin+)

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member

409.description: Email is already a member or already invited

post.summary: Issue a pairing code for the calling account
Exchange a pairing code for a session (used by the TUI)
One-time onboarding for an account created via OAuth
post.description: Atomically sets the recovery key, the initial encrypted vault and (optionally) a password auth key — all-or-nothing, so an account can never end up with a recovery key but no vault. Strictly first-time: rejected with 409 if a recovery key or vault already exists, or if authKey is supplied while a password is already set. Rotation stays exclusive to recover/reset.

409.description: Recovery key or vault already exists, or password already set

post.description: In COOKIE mode the refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie; in DIRECT mode it is returned in the body.

post.summary: Exchange a refresh token for a new access token
post.description: Reads the refresh token from the httpOnly cookie when present, otherwise from the body.

401.description: Missing, invalid or revoked refresh token

post.summary: Verify a recovery code and return the vault for re-encryption
Re-encrypt the vault under a new password and rotate the recovery code
post.description: Atomically replaces the credential hashes and vault, invalidates the old recovery code and revokes all existing sessions. In COOKIE mode the refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie; in DIRECT mode it is returned in the body.

post.summary: Rotate the master-password auth key and re-encrypt the vault
post.description: Authenticated master-password change: verifies the current auth key, replaces the stored auth-key hash with one derived from the new password and stores the vault blob re-encrypted under it (bumping the vault version). The recovery code is left untouched, and existing sessions stay valid (device pairings are independent of the password). Requires a password to already be set — OAuth-first accounts set one via /auth/setup.

200.description: Password changed; new vault version returned

409.description: No password is set for this account

401.description: Not authenticated, or the current auth key does not match

post.summary: Authenticate with the derived auth key
post.description: In COOKIE mode the refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie; in DIRECT mode it is returned in the body.

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member
Project not found or caller is not a member

patch.summary: Update a project's name and/or description (admin+)

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member

patch.summary: Change a member's role (owner only; OWNER transfers ownership)

400.description: Owner cannot demote themselves without a transfer

404.description: Project or target member not found

get.description: Includes hasPassword / hasRecovery / hasVault flags and the account's public key.

get.summary: List unexpired project invites addressed to the caller
List members awaiting a key who have published a public key (admin+)

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member

get.description: Validates and consumes the state, exchanges the code server-to-server, requires a verified email, links/creates the local account, sets the httpOnly refresh cookie and 302-redirects to /oauth/complete. On any failure it redirects to /oauth/complete?error=<code> (provider_disabled | invalid_state | email_not_verified | provider_error | server_error).

302.description: Redirect to /oauth/complete on success (with refresh cookie) or /oauth/complete?error=<code> on failure

get.description: 302-redirects to the provider's consent page with a one-time state. A disabled/unknown provider redirects to /oauth/complete?error=provider_disabled.

302.description: Redirect to the provider consent page (or to /oauth/complete on error)

get.description: Returns the slugs of providers configured with a client id + secret; empty when none are configured.

delete.summary: Leave a project (the owner must transfer ownership first)

404.description: Project not found or caller is not a member

409.description: Owner cannot leave without transferring ownership

expectedVersion.description: The version the client last saw; the write is rejected (409) if it has moved on

UpdateVaultRequest.description: Replace the vault blob with optimistic concurrency

VaultUpdateResponse.description: The new version after a successful vault write

rotate.description: When true, replace an existing key and reset every wrapped key the account holds (each affected project membership re-enters the awaiting-key state)

UpdatePublicKeyRequest.description: Publish or rotate the account's X25519 public key

role.description: The calling member's role in this project

awaitingKey.description: True when the caller holds no wrapped key yet (awaiting an admin to seal the DEK)

ProjectSummary.description: A project as it appears in the caller's project list

wrappedDek.description: Base64-encoded project DEK sealed to the owner's public key (exactly 80 bytes)

CreateProjectRequest.description: Create a project with its initial encrypted vault and the owner's wrapped DEK

role.description: The calling member's role in this project

Project.description: Full project detail: metadata, members and pending invites

ProjectInvite.description: A pending invite on a project

keyed.description: Whether the DEK has been sealed to this member's public key

publicKey.description: The member's base64 X25519 public key, or null if they have not published one

removeUserId.description: Optional member to remove as part of the rotation

vault.description: Base64-encoded ciphertext project vault blob, re-encrypted under the new DEK

expectedVersion.description: The version the client last saw; the rotation is rejected (409) if it has moved on

wrappedKeys.description: The new DEK wrapped once per remaining member

RotateProjectRequest.description: Rotate the project DEK and re-wrap it for all remaining members

userId.description: The member the key is wrapped for

wrappedDek.description: Base64-encoded DEK sealed to that member's public key (exactly 80 bytes)

WrappedKeyEntry.description: A member's copy of the new DEK, sealed to their public key

wrappedDek.description: Base64-encoded project DEK sealed to the target member's public key (exactly 80 bytes)

vaultVersion.description: The project vault version the DEK was wrapped against; rejected (409) if the vault has since rotated, so a stale DEK is never accepted

SubmitWrappedKeyRequest.description: Seal the project DEK to a member's public key

CreateInviteRequest.description: Invite an email address to a project

code.description: The pairing code; dashes and case are normalised

deviceName.description: Optional human-readable name for the device

DeviceCodeExchangeRequest.description: Exchange a pairing code for a TUI session

SessionResponse.description: The account plus tokens for a new session (login / device-code exchange). In COOKIE mode the refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie and omitted here.

recoveryAuthKey.description: base64 HKDF-derived recovery authentication key for the freshly generated recovery code

vault.description: Base64-encoded initial ciphertext vault blob (WHARFV format)

authKey.description: Optional base64 HKDF-derived authentication key; when present the account also gains password login (rejected if a password is already set)

AccountSetupRequest.description: One-time onboarding for an account created via OAuth: atomically sets the recovery key, the initial encrypted vault, and (optionally) the password auth key derived from the same master password the vault was encrypted with

authKey.description: base64 HKDF-derived authentication key (never the password)

tokenMode.description: Token delivery mode; defaults to COOKIE for browsers

AuthResponse.description: The account plus a new token pair (register / recovery reset). In COOKIE mode the refresh token is set as an httpOnly cookie and omitted from the pair.

accessToken.description: Short-lived identity (access) token; send as Bearer

refreshToken.description: Long-lived refresh token; present only in DIRECT mode (in COOKIE mode it is set as an httpOnly cookie and omitted here)

TokenPair.description: A freshly issued identity + refresh token pair

refreshToken.description: Refresh token; required in DIRECT mode, ignored if a cookie is present

tokenMode.description: Token delivery mode; defaults to COOKIE

RefreshRequest.description: Optional body for refresh. In COOKIE mode the refresh token is read from the cookie instead.

refreshToken.description: Rotated refresh token; present only in DIRECT mode

AccessTokenResponse.description: A refreshed access token (and rotated refresh in DIRECT mode)

RecoveryVerifyRequest.description: Verify a recovery code and retrieve the vault for re-encryption

RecoveryVerifyResponse.description: The current vault blob, so the browser can decrypt it via the recovery slot

recoveryAuthKey.description: The current recovery authentication key (proves ownership)

newAuthKey.description: New base64 authentication key derived from the new password

newRecoveryAuthKey.description: New base64 recovery authentication key for the freshly issued recovery code

vault.description: Base64-encoded vault blob re-encrypted with the new key material

tokenMode.description: Token delivery mode; defaults to COOKIE for browsers

RecoveryResetRequest.description: Re-encrypt the vault under a new password and rotate the recovery code

currentAuthKey.description: The current base64 authentication key (proves the caller knows the current password)

newAuthKey.description: New base64 authentication key derived from the new password

vault.description: Base64-encoded vault blob re-encrypted under the new password (recovery slot unchanged)

ChangePasswordRequest.description: Rotate the master-password auth key and re-encrypt the vault under the new password. The recovery code is unchanged, so its slot in the vault blob stays valid.

tokenMode.description: Token delivery mode; defaults to COOKIE for browsers

name.description: New name, or null to leave unchanged

description.description: New description, or null to leave unchanged

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Patch a project's name and/or description

role.description: The new role. Setting a member to OWNER transfers ownership and demotes the current owner to ADMIN.

VaultResponse.description: The stored ciphertext vault and its version

hasPassword.description: Whether a master-password auth key is set (false for a fresh OAuth account)

hasRecovery.description: Whether a recovery key is set

hasVault.description: Whether an encrypted vault exists for this account

publicKey.description: The account's base64 X25519 public key, or null if none is published yet

UserProfile.description: The authenticated account's profile plus routing capability flags

MyInvite.description: An invite addressed to the calling account

wrappedDek.description: The caller's base64 wrapped DEK, or null if they are still awaiting a key

ProjectVaultResponse.description: The project's ciphertext vault plus the caller's wrapped DEK

publicKey.description: The member's base64 X25519 public key to seal the DEK against

PendingKey.description: A member awaiting a wrapped key who has published a public key to seal against

providers.description: Enabled provider slugs, e.g. ["google","github"]; empty when none are configured

items.description: Enabled provider slugs, e.g. ["google","github"]; empty when none are configured

OAuthProvidersResponse.description: The OAuth providers that are currently enabled (configured with a client id + secret)
